title: D&D 5e 2024 Services
description: Complete list of services, lifestyle expenses, and hireling costs

lifestyles.name: Wretched
lifestyles.description: You survive via chance and charity. You're often exposed to natural dangers as a result of sleeping outside.

lifestyles.name: Squalid
lifestyles.description: You spend the bare minimum for your necessities. You might be exposed to unhealthy conditions and opportunistic criminals.

lifestyles.name: Poor
lifestyles.description: You spend frugally for your necessities.

lifestyles.name: Modest
lifestyles.description: You support yourself at an average level.

lifestyles.name: Comfortable
lifestyles.description: You spend modestly for your necessities and enjoy a few luxuries.

lifestyles.name: Wealthy
lifestyles.description: You're accustomed to the finer things in life and might have servants.

lifestyles.name: Aristocratic
lifestyles.description: You pay for the best and might have a staff that supports your lifestyle. Others notice your wealth and might encourage you to share it, either legally or otherwise.

hirelings.description: Anyone hired to perform a service that involves a proficiency: a mercenary, an artisan, a scribe, etc.
hirelings.note: This is a minimum; some expert hirelings require more pay

hirelings.description: Hired for work that requires no particular proficiencies; includes laborers and porters
Hired to deliver messages or small items

notes.lifestyles: At the start of each week or month (DM's choice), choose a lifestyle and pay the price to sustain it. Lifestyles have no inherent consequences, but the DM might take them into account when determining risks or how others perceive your character.
notes.spellcasting: If a spell has expensive components, add the cost of those components to the listed cost. The higher the level of a desired spell, the harder it is to find someone to cast it.
notes.foodAndLodging: Prices for daily lodging and meals are included in your lifestyle's expenses.
